artifact.feature_name: Financial Telemetry Phase 1.3 — UI Layer: Split Display + Labels

surfaces: components/table/rundown-summary-panel.tsx — wrap fills/credits/drop/win-loss; Pattern B for derived
components/cashier/cash-out-form.tsx — wrap committed; Draft badge for pre-commit
app/(landing)/floor-oversight/page.tsx — Coverage quality→Attribution Ratio; route through <AttributionRatio>

coverage_mode.full_mode_justification: Dedicated Presentation rollout slice per ROLLOUT-ROADMAP.md §3 Phase 1.3. Representative coverage insufficient: Phase 1.4 lint enforcement requires 100% migration before activation; partial migration fails Phase 1.3 exit gate 'no unlabeled currency in any rendered surface'. Surface inventory is bounded and fixed (Phase 1.0). GOV-FIB-001 §6.3 explicitly permits Full mode for dedicated rollout/inventory slices that do not also introduce new service semantics, transport shape, or infrastructure. This slice is UI-layer semantic enforcement of the already-stable upstream FinancialValue contract. Legacy shift-dashboard (components/shift-dashboard/) and shift-dashboard-v2 (app/review/shift-dashboard-v2/) are confirmed dead code and excluded from scope.

layer_budget.secondary_layer_constraints: none — no service, API, or infrastructure layer receives logic changes
layer_budget.dto_classification: no DTO changes in this slice — service output is stable from Phase 1.2B

scope_guardrail.one_line_boundary: This FIB normalizes existing financial UI surfaces so stable FinancialValue envelopes are rendered truthfully to operators by introducing shared display components and migrating all in-scope surface families; it does not change service output, API contracts, lint enforcement rules, or observability infrastructure.
scope_guardrail.migration_posture: This phase is not feature delivery. It is a surface normalization migration and UI-layer semantic enforcement of an upstream contract. Review priority is consistency, uniformity, and semantic preservation over new UI flexibility or clever component abstraction. Reviewers must not treat this as cosmetic-only UI work: forbidden label correction, authority correction, completeness correction, and degradation behavior require semantic discipline.

atomicity_test.atomicity_notes: After Phase 1.3, every PT-2 surface shows labeled financial values. Phase 1.4 lint rules and Playwright tests are independent tooling on the stable migrated surface set, but they must encode the semantic constraints established here to prevent regression. Component births without surface migrations would be truthfully incomplete (new primitives with no consumers). Full migration ships as a correct, internally consistent operator experience with sentinel source-of-truth validation for each authority class.

spot_check_matrix: Mixed Actual + Estimated surface → degrades to Estimated
Compliance surface → remains isolated and is never merged with operational authorities
Unknown completeness surface → explicitly renders unknown / Not computed instead of $0, blank, or inferred completeness
semantic_alignment.sentinel_correctness_gate: For each authority class (actual, estimated, observed, compliance), at least one migrated surface must be validated against source-of-truth data. This is a sentinel check, not full semantic coverage.

diff_size_sanity.multi_boundary_justification: Scale exceeds §11 single-boundary default. Justified as dedicated full-inventory Presentation rollout phase (GOV-FIB-001 §6.3). Every file touched: replace bare currency display with <FinancialValue> call. No file requires knowledge of another file's logic beyond the shared component API. Scale is linear across surface families. Splitting component-birth from surface-migration produces an intermediate state where new components exist with zero consumers — fails §6.7 'ships as correct internal contract'. Surface inventory is bounded by Phase 1.0. Dead code (legacy shift-dashboard, shift-dashboard-v2) excluded — scope reduced accordingly.

adjacent_consequence_ledger.disposition: Phase 1.4 — must encode Phase 1.3 authority ambiguity, hidden/unknown completeness, degradation behavior, and no-compliance-mixing constraints

adjacent_consequence_ledger.temptation: Playwright DOM assertions: no currency rendered without visible authority label
adjacent_consequence_ledger.why_adjacent: Automated coverage of migrated surfaces; depends on stable component API
Natural after surfaces, components, and lint rules are all stable
Validates completeness rendering; depends on stable <FinancialValue> component
<FinancialValue> uses these internally; removal reduces duplication
adjacent_consequence_ledger.disposition: Phase 1.4 — removal requires lint guard first; breaking exports in Phase 1.3 makes migration order fragile

adjacent_consequence_ledger.temptation: Attribution Ratio value computation logic changes
adjacent_consequence_ledger.why_adjacent: Label rename makes computation visibility natural next step
adjacent_consequence_ledger.disposition: Out of scope — service-layer concern; <AttributionRatio> renders what service provides

adjacent_consequence_ledger.temptation: Structured log events per forbidden-label usage in production
adjacent_consequence_ledger.why_adjacent: Adjacent while touching surface files containing forbidden labels
adjacent_consequence_ledger.disposition: Phase 1.4 — Observability class; different change class from Presentation

phase_boundary.one_line_invariant: If a change births a shared financial display component, freezes the FinancialValue props contract, migrates a surface from bare formatter to <FinancialValue>, removes a forbidden label, aligns authority/completeness semantics, or births a DEF-006 component test — and nothing else — it is in scope.
stop_conditions: Playwright test suite authoring required beyond DEF-006
average_bet, policy thresholds, or loyalty points wrapped as FinancialValue
prohibited: ESLint rule authoring (Phase 1.4 — must encode authority ambiguity, hidden/unknown completeness, degradation behavior, and no compliance mixing)
average_bet, min_bet, max_bet, watchlistFloor, ctrThreshold wrapping (WAVE-1-CLASSIFICATION-RULES §6)
Attribution Ratio value computation changes (service-layer concern)

intent.operator_problem: Pit bosses and floor supervisors see bare currency values across PT-2 UI surfaces with no visible authority label. Some surfaces carry forbidden labels (Handle, Coverage quality) or placeholder zeros (Theo: 0) that misrepresent source authority. The operator cannot assess confidence in any displayed financial number. This violates SRC §L1 (every rendered currency value must declare its authority visibly) and blocks Wave 1 sign-off.
intent.pilot_fit: Labeled financial surfaces are a Wave 1 exit criterion. Service and API layers have fully propagated the FinancialValue envelope (Phases 1.1–1.2B). UI is the last unlabeled boundary. DEF-006 component test births are also a Phase 1.3 obligation deferred from Phase 1.1 due to DTO shape changes that stabilized in Phase 1.2B.

entities.name: FinancialValue.tsx
entities.description: New shared rendering primitive. Renders { value, type, source, completeness } with visible authority badge (SRC §L2 — badge visible at first glance, not tooltip-only). Internal use of lib/format.ts formatCents for number formatting.

entities.name: AttributionRatio.tsx
entities.description: New KPI component for Attribution Ratio (SRC §K1 rename from 'Coverage'). Renders Rated / (Rated + Estimated) ratio as a percentage with appropriate label. Does not compute the ratio — renders what the service provides.

entities.name: CompletenessBadge.tsx
entities.description: New badge component rendering completeness status: complete | partial | unknown. Visual weight differentiated per status. Used inside FinancialValue.tsx and standalone for completeness-only surfaces.

entities.name: types/financial.ts
entities.description: Existing type source. FinancialValue, FinancialAuthority, CompletenessStatus. No changes in Phase 1.3 — consumed by new components.

entities.name: 11 in-scope UI surface families
entities.description: Existing surface files enumerated in WAVE-1-SURFACE-INVENTORY.md §5.2. Each family migrated from bare formatters / local label strings to <FinancialValue> component. See scope_guardrail.coverage_mode.surfaces for the complete concrete list. Legacy shift-dashboard and shift-dashboard-v2 are excluded (confirmed dead code).

entities.name: DEF-006 component tests
entities.description: Three test files born in Phase 1.3: rating-slip-modal.test.tsx, start-from-previous.test.tsx, start-from-previous-modal.test.tsx. Assert DTO shape from Phase 1.1/1.2B against component renders.

boundaries: No service layer changes — Phase 1.2B complete
No API / OpenAPI changes — Phase 1.2B-C complete
No Playwright DOM assertion suite — Phase 1.4
average_bet, policy thresholds, loyalty points not wrapped (WAVE-1-CLASSIFICATION-RULES §6)
lib/format.ts exported helpers unchanged — used internally by <FinancialValue>
Legacy shift-dashboard (components/shift-dashboard/) — dead code, no action

capabilities.name: Shared financial display component births
capabilities.description: Birth FinancialValue.tsx, AttributionRatio.tsx, CompletenessBadge.tsx in components/financial/. Each component consumes types/financial.ts types. FinancialValue renders value + type badge + completeness indicator with SRC §L2 visibility rule enforced structurally.
capabilities.output: Shared rendering primitives available for all surface migrations

capabilities.name: FinancialValue props contract freeze
capabilities.description: Freeze the <FinancialValue> props contract before rollout. Allowed combinations: one authority class per render; unknown completeness must render explicitly; compliance cannot be accepted in combined/mixed variants; mixed actual/observed/estimated displays must declare degraded display authority and not hide contributing authorities.
capabilities.output: Phase 1.4 lint targets a stable component API and the migration does not rely on drifting manual conventions

capabilities.output: shift-dashboard-v3 surface family fully labeled; dead code excluded from scope

Enforce semantic spot-check matrix: actual-only stays Actual; mixed Actual + Estimated degrades to Estimated; Compliance remains isolated; unknown completeness renders explicitly. Validate at least one surface per authority class against source-of-truth data as a sentinel correctness check. Lead-architect sign-off on all labeled surfaces. Run npm run type-check + npm run lint + npm run build.

trigger_events.description: Phase 1.2B exit gate passed (all sub-phases closed, commits ad6d1748 / e73879f4, 2026-05-03): service output is integer-cents FinancialValue; API contract stable; BRIDGE-001 retired. UI is the last unlabeled boundary.
Wave 1 exit criterion 1 ('SRC envelope present on every production financial surface — API + UI') and criterion 5 ('Attribution Ratio renders correctly') cannot be met without UI surface migration.

lifecycle_events: All 11 in-scope surface families migrated to <FinancialValue>
Forbidden labels removed from production UI
DEF-006 component tests born and passing
Phase 1.3 exit gate met — Phase 1.4 entry unblocked
timing_constraints: Must complete before Phase 1.4 can begin (lint rules require stable component API)
Must complete before Wave 1 sign-off criterion 1 and 5 can be checked

business_rules.statement: Every rendered currency value must carry a visible authority label (actual | estimated | observed | compliance) at first glance — not tooltip-only (SRC §L2). FinancialValue.tsx enforces this structurally.

Forbidden labels must not appear in production UI: Handle (→ Estimated Drop), Coverage quality (→ Attribution Ratio), Theo: 0 (→ unknown envelope + Not computed badge). See WAVE-1-FORBIDDEN-LABELS §2.A–E.

Pattern A split display (SRC §8) must be applied wherever rated (actual) and unrated (estimated) values appear together. Mixed-class aggregates degrade to lowest authority present (D5: Actual > Observed > Estimated).
Compliance authority (compliance) must never be merged with actual, estimated, or observed in a single aggregate display. MTL surfaces must hold compliance amounts in their own component.
hold_percent is never wrapped as FinancialValue. It is a dimensionless ratio — bare number. DEF-NEVER hard invariant survives all phases.

average_bet, policy thresholds (min_bet, max_bet, watchlistFloor, ctrThreshold), loyalty points, segment average_bet are not wrapped as FinancialValue per WAVE-1-CLASSIFICATION-RULES §6. Operator inputs remain bare numbers; points are a separate unit system.

FinancialValue.value is always integer cents at the service boundary. UI components must use formatCents (not formatDollars) for rendering. DEF-004 already migrated start-from-previous.tsx in Phase 1.2B-B — Phase 1.3 must not reintroduce formatDollars on FinancialValue fields.
DEF-006 component tests must be born before Phase 1.3 closes. They assert integer-cents FinancialValue shape on fields changed in Phases 1.1/1.2B.

The <FinancialValue> props contract must be frozen before rollout. Allowed combinations are documented before surface migration so Phase 1.4 lint has a stable target and Phase 1.3 does not introduce drift.
Semantic spot-check matrix must pass during walkthrough: actual-only stays Actual; mixed Actual + Estimated degrades to Estimated; Compliance is never merged; unknown completeness renders explicitly.

For each authority class (actual, estimated, observed, compliance), at least one migrated surface must be validated against source-of-truth data as a sentinel correctness check. This is not full semantic coverage.

invariants: hold_percent is never FinancialValue in any UI surface (DEF-NEVER)
FinancialValue.value is integer cents — never dollar-float (BRIDGE-001 retired Phase 1.2B-A)
Compliance authority is never merged with actual/estimated/observed in a single aggregate (SRC §C1)
Authority labels are visible at first glance — not tooltip-only (SRC §L2)
Completeness status is always declared (complete | partial | unknown) — never omitted, never guessed complete (SRC §L1)
No service or API logic changes in this phase — FinancialValue envelope shape is stable from Phase 1.2B
decision_notes: Legacy shift-dashboard (components/shift-dashboard/) excluded: confirmed dead code — zero UI imports (grep verified 2026-05-03); production route app/(protected)/shift-dashboard/page.tsx imports ShiftDashboardV3 exclusively.
shift-dashboard-v2 (app/review/shift-dashboard-v2/**) excluded: dev preview, dead code per owner confirmation (2026-05-03).

Pre-commit form inputs (cashier cash-out-form, rating slip buy-in inputs) are Draft-labeled, not FinancialValue-wrapped, per WAVE-1-CLASSIFICATION-RULES §6.1. 'Draft' badge is the correct UI treatment until the input is committed.

buy-in-threshold-indicator.tsx consumer pattern: the component is passed currency as a prop; Phase 1.3 ensures callers pass a typed FinancialValue envelope rather than a bare number. The component reads .type and .source for display.

Phase 1.3 defines the law but not the police: semantic constraints are enforced through component API discipline and manual walkthrough now; Phase 1.4 must encode them in lint/tests to prevent regression.
